name: Basta
classification_notes: The presence of small caps, italics, and a range from Light to Black with classic weight distribution suggests a text-oriented transitional or humanist serif intended for extended reading.
summary: A text serif from Typemanufactur offered in four weights with italics and small caps across nine styles.
description: Basta runs from Light to Black with accompanying italics at Light, Regular and Bold weights, plus dedicated small caps variants for those three. The range of nine styles makes it well suited to book and editorial work where typographic hierarchy and long-form readability matter. The literary specimen text choices suggest a typeface positioned for serious prose contexts.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Basta as a serif family available in nine styles: Light, Light Italic, Light Small Caps, Regular, Regular Italic, Regular Small Caps, Bold, Bold Italic and Black.

name: Brasil
classification_notes: Brasil presents classical serif proportions with small caps and a full italic across a wide weight range, placing it firmly in the transitional serif tradition suited to text and editorial use.
summary: A six-weight serif family from Typemanufactur with small caps throughout and a full italic, suited to long-form editorial and book work.
description: Seventeen styles covering six weights from Extralight to Black, each with italic and small caps variants except Black which carries italic alone. The family is built around sustained reading, with small caps integrated at every weight for a level of typographic refinement rarely offered below the heavier cuts. Works well in literary, magazine and book contexts where weight contrast and classical forms are expected.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Brasil as a serif family spanning Extralight to Black with italics and small caps across the range, offering a versatile toolkit for editorial and text-setting applications.

name: Axiom
classification_notes: The presence of small caps, italics, and two optical size subfamilies (11 and 66) points to a text-oriented contemporary serif designed for sustained reading and editorial use across scales.
summary: A contemporary serif with two optical sizes, small caps and italics across four weights, suited to sustained editorial and reading contexts.
description: Axiom is structured around two optical size subfamilies: Axiom 11, tuned for smaller text settings, and Axiom 66, intended for display or headline use. Each subfamily offers Light, Regular, Bold and Black weights, with italics and small caps variants rounding out the range. The family is clearly positioned for editorial and typographic work that demands legibility and formal control at both text and display sizes.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Axiom as an 18-style serif family split across two optical sizes, Axiom 11 and Axiom 66, with each offering Light, Regular, Bold and Black weights alongside italic and small caps cuts for refined typographic flexibility.

name: Dalli
classification_notes: Dalli is a connected handwritten script family offered in two subfamilies, Script and Forte, typical of casual calligraphic display typefaces.
summary: A connected handwritten script family in two variants, Script and Forte, suited to display and editorial use.
description: Dalli comes in two subfamilies: Dalli Script and Dalli Forte, offering different weights or treatments of the same connected handwritten style. The family reads warmly at display sizes and works well for headlines, packaging and short passages of expressive text. The page content suggests a casual, flowing character suited to editorial contexts where personality is required.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Dalli as a script family with two subfamilies, Dalli Script and Dalli Forte, demonstrated across a range of editorial and display contexts.

name: Carree
classification_notes: Carree is a geometric, square-structured display typeface with variants including outline and panel cuts, placing it firmly in the display category with a geometric character.
summary: A square-natured geometric display family from Typemanufactur, offered in six variants including outline and panel cuts.
description: Built on a rigid geometric framework, this family spans three weights from Light to Bold alongside a wide variant, an outline cut and a panel version. The outline and panel styles give designers options for layering and decorative applications. Best suited to headlines, logotypes and display work where a strong, constructed geometry is required.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Carree across six styles: Light, Light Wide, Regular, Bold, Outline and Panel, offering a versatile range of options within a square geometric design system.

name: Daphne
classification_notes: Daphne presents as a text serif with classical proportions suited to extended reading, consistent with a transitional or humanist serif classification based on the specimen's rendering at various sizes.
summary: A single-weight text serif from Typemanufactur, shown across literary and editorial specimen settings.
description: Daphne is offered in a Regular weight, demonstrated through extended prose passages that suggest its primary orientation toward text-heavy editorial and literary use. The specimen favours long-form copy, from Pynchon to Louise Erdrich, implying a typeface built for sustained reading rather than display. Only one weight is shown on the page, giving it a restrained, focused character.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Daphne as an editable, versatile text typeface, demonstrated through a series of literary quotations and extended prose samples that highlight its performance at running text sizes.

name: Daphnescript Regular
classification_notes: Daphnescript is a formal cursive script typeface, consistent with the calligraphic script tradition used for editorial, packaging and luxury applications.
summary: A single-weight formal script from Typemanufactur, suited to editorial, packaging and luxury branding contexts.
description: A calligraphic formal script offered in a single Regular weight. The page specimens suggest fluid, connected letterforms typical of the formal script tradition, lending themselves to packaging, invitations and editorial headlines. The limited weight range keeps the family focused and purposeful rather than broadly versatile.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Daphnescript as a formal script typeface, demonstrated through specimen settings including short literary titles and a longer prose passage, showing its legibility across both display and running text contexts.

name: Deutschkurrent
classification_notes: Kurrent is a cursive form of German blackletter script used historically for everyday handwriting; Deutschkurrent directly references and revives this tradition.
summary: A revival of the traditional German Kurrent cursive script, rendered as a single Regular weight.
description: Deutschkurrent draws on the historic German Kurrent handwriting script, a flowing cursive form of blackletter that was used as everyday script in German-speaking regions for centuries. The specimen text, featuring German phrases and poetry, underlines its affinity for period-appropriate and culturally specific typographic applications. Available in a single Regular weight, it suits decorative, heritage and editorial contexts where authentic German script character is required.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Deutschkurrent as a typeface rooted in the traditional German Kurrent cursive script, demonstrated through specimen text drawn from German poetry and everyday phrases.

name: Essenz
classification_notes: The letterforms and italic cuts suggest a humanist sans construction with warm, legible proportions suited to both text and display use across a range of weights.
summary: A five-weight humanist sans from Typemanufactur with matching italics across each weight, suited to sustained text setting and editorial work.
description: Essenz offers ten styles spanning Light through Black, each paired with an italic, giving the family good range for both body text and display use. The humanist construction keeps it readable at smaller sizes while the bolder weights carry enough presence for headlines. A reliable workhorse without strong personality, aimed at professional publishing and editorial contexts.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Essenz as a family of ten styles running from Light to Black with accompanying italics, designed for versatile use across a range of typographic contexts.

name: Gordon
classification_notes: The presence of small caps, italics, and a structured weight range alongside the formal text specimens suggests a transitional or classical serif design suited to literary and editorial setting.
summary: A three-weight serif with small caps and italics, designed for sustained text setting in literary and editorial contexts.
description: Gordon offers Light, Regular and Bold weights, each with matching italics and small caps, giving it a modest but complete set of tools for book and editorial work. The specimen texts drawn from literary sources suggest an intent towards long-form reading. Without condensed or display cuts, it sits squarely as a text serif rather than a versatile family.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Gordon as a serif family available in Light, Regular and Bold, with italic and small caps variants across the range.

name: Magnet
classification_notes: The specimen treatments and expressive headline usage suggest a display-oriented serif designed for large-scale editorial and poster work rather than text setting.
summary: A display serif from Typemanufactur suited to headline and poster work with an expressive, confident character.
description: Shown in a single Regular weight, Magnet is presented through bold editorial settings ranging from lyrical literary text to stark all-caps headlines. Its specimen pairings suggest a typeface built for large-scale display use where personality matters more than neutrality. The limited weight offering keeps the focus on the cut itself rather than a broad family system.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Magnet through a series of expressive specimen settings, demonstrating its versatility across editorial headlines, poster copy and short-form display text.

name: Planet
classification_notes: Planet is a contemporary serif with small caps support and a broad weight range from Light to Extrablack, suggesting a text and display hybrid with classical serif structure and modern proportions.
summary: A contemporary serif family from Typemanufactur spanning six weights from Light to Extrablack, with italics and small caps across the lighter cuts.
description: Planet covers a wide tonal range, from delicate Light through to a heavy Extrablack, making it suitable for both running text and display settings. Small caps are available in Light, Regular and Medium, adding typographic depth for editorial and book work. Italics accompany the three lightest weights, giving the family a classical structure while the heavier ends push toward expressive use.
foundry_blurb: Planet is offered by Typemanufactur as a serif family of thirteen styles, including light, regular, medium, bold, black and extrablack weights, with italic and small caps variants across the lighter cuts, suited to a range of typographic applications from text to display.

name: Tap
classification_notes: The page presents a clean, upright sans-serif with no visible serifs and a straightforward weight range, consistent with a contemporary grotesque or neo-grotesque classification.
summary: A five-weight grotesque sans from Typemanufactur, spanning Extralight to Black for text and editorial use.
description: Tap offers a straightforward weight range from Extralight through to Black, without condensed or italic variants. The family reads as a clean, neutral grotesque suited to body text and display settings alike. With no ornamentation or stylistic flourishes, it sits comfortably in editorial and interface work where legibility takes precedence.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Tap as a grotesque sans available in five weights: Extralight, Light, Regular, Bold and Black, offered through their type catalogue.

name: Rolls
classification_notes: The even stroke contrast, neutral letterforms and range of weights from Light to Black place Rolls firmly in the grotesque sans-serif tradition, suitable for editorial and display use alike.
summary: A five-weight grotesque sans from Typemanufactur, each weight paired with an italic, suited to editorial and identity work.
description: Ten styles span five weights from Light to Black, each accompanied by a matching italic. The family reads cleanly at text sizes and carries enough presence for display settings, making it a workable choice for editorial design, branding and cultural institutions. Its tone is controlled and neutral without being anonymous.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Rolls as a grotesque sans-serif available in five weights with italics, from Light through to Black, offering a range that covers both text and display applications.

name: Polo
classification_notes: The family's broad range of optical sizes, widths and weights, alongside its clean upright structure with no visible serif detail, places it firmly in the grotesque sans-serif tradition. The numbered optical size variants (11, 22, 66) and compressed/condensed widths are characteristic of a systematic, text-oriented grotesque.
summary: A large-system grotesque from Typemanufactur, structured across three optical sizes, multiple widths and five weights with matching italics and a smallcaps subfamily.
description: Three numbered optical variants — 11, 22 and 66 — anchor the family for different point sizes and output conditions, each running from Light to Black with italics throughout. Width variants extend from Extracondensed through Condensed and Compressed, broadening the palette for poster and tight-column work. A dedicated Smallcaps subfamily and two display-weight singles, Extralight and Fino, round out a system built for sustained typographic use.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Polo as an extensive type system offering optical sizes (Polo 11, Polo 22, Polo 66), multiple widths including Condensed, Compressed and Extracondensed, a Smallcaps subfamily, and a wide weight range from Light to Black, with italics throughout.

name: Videon
classification_notes: Videon has a clean, constructed geometric character with a technical, machine-like quality, placing it firmly in the geometric sans-serif tradition.
summary: A four-weight geometric sans with a technical, machine-like character from Typemanufactur.
description: Four weights from Light to Bold make up this geometric sans, which carries a constructed, technical quality suited to design contexts that want precision and clarity. The specimen language — 'machine language' and 'Mathematical beauty' — signals its intended register. No italics or condensed cuts are offered, keeping the family tight and purposeful.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Videon as a geometric sans available in four weights: Light, Regular, Medium and Bold, with specimen copy evoking machine language and mathematical beauty.

name: Turbo
classification_notes: The even stroke weight, upright letterforms and restrained construction suggest a neo-grotesque or contemporary grotesque sans-serif. The range of weights and the italic variants are consistent with a text-oriented grotesque family.
summary: A five-weight grotesque sans with matching italics, suited to editorial and text-setting work across a range of typographic contexts.
description: Turbo spans five weights from Light to Black, each with a corresponding italic, giving it a practical range for both body text and display use. The family's even construction and clean structure place it in the contemporary grotesque tradition. With ten styles in total, it covers most demands in editorial, identity and screen-based work.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur offer Turbo across ten styles, from Light to Black with matching italics, presenting it as a versatile grotesque sans suited to a broad range of typographic applications.

name: Votum
classification_notes: The presence of small caps, italic variants, and a range from Light to Black across text-oriented weights suggests a classical text serif tradition consistent with transitional serif conventions, designed for sustained reading.
summary: A text serif from Typemanufactur offered in four weights with italics and small caps across eleven styles.
description: Four weights from Light to Black each come with matching italics, and the three lighter weights gain small cap variants, giving eleven styles in total. The family is pitched at extended reading: book, editorial and long-form contexts where small caps and a measured weight range matter. No condensed or display cuts are offered, keeping the family focused on text work.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Votum as a serif family spanning Light, Regular, Bold and Black, with italics and small caps throughout, suited to demanding text settings.

name: Zitat
classification_notes: The letterforms visible in the specimen suggest a transitional serif with moderate contrast and upright stress, suited to text setting in both roman and italic cuts.
summary: A compact three-style text serif from Typemanufactur, offered in Regular, Regular Italic and Bold.
description: A modest text serif with Regular, Italic and Bold cuts, suited to body copy and editorial work. The specimen text leans on literary sources, suggesting an orientation towards book and long-form typesetting. With only three styles on offer, the family is narrow in scope but legible in intent.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Zitat as a text serif available in three styles: Regular, Regular Italic and Bold, with specimen copy drawn from literary sources to demonstrate its suitability for extended reading.

name: Wasenbacher
classification_notes: The sample text and styling are characteristic of a German blackletter tradition, with the typeface name and specimen text evoking regional German vernacular lettering.
summary: A single-weight blackletter drawing on German vernacular lettering traditions, suited to display and heritage applications.
description: A blackletter typeface from Typemanufactur with a distinctly regional German character, as suggested by the specimen texts drawn from German folk poetry and signage. Available in a single Regular weight, it is positioned for display use where a traditional or historical tone is required. The limited weight range keeps it focused and deliberate rather than a utility workhorse.
foundry_blurb: Typemanufactur present Wasenbacher as a blackletter typeface, showcasing it through classic German-language specimen text including poetry and period-appropriate signage phrases, emphasising its traditional character.
